title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency relating to "Quality Control Standards for Automated Valuation Models".
plain_summary: Congress is rejecting a rule made by a government agency that watches over banks. The rule was about computer programs that banks use to figure out how much houses and other properties are worth. Congress has the power to stop rules made by government agencies if they don't like them. This resolution uses that power to prevent this particular banking rule from becoming official.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress disapproves of a rule that was submitted by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency. This rule was about Quality Control Standards for Automated Valuation Models. The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency is a government agency that oversees national banks. Automated Valuation Models are computer programs that estimate how much properties are worth. This rule was published in the Federal Register on August seventh, two thousand twenty four. Because Congress disapproves of this rule, it will have no power and will not take effect.
